title: Investment operations specialist
change: Occupational standard and assessment plan revised.
overviewOfRole: Carry out investment transactions on behalf of individuals or organisations

knowledges.detail: The financial services industry, the role and purpose of markets, and the process of investing relevant to their role.
The function in which they work; how this role relates to other functions within their department, the wider business, and the broader investment markets.
The investment lifecycle, including trade processing, settlement, reconciliation, corporate actions, and custody.
The organisation's aims, objectives, and how the investment operations function contributes to them.

UK financial regulations and legislation, including the industry regulators, for example, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) or the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA), Consumer Duty, Client Assets Sourcebook (CASS) rules, anti-money laundering (AML) regulations, and data protection laws relevant to the investment role.

Organisational policies and procedures related to compliance, risk management, breach reporting, and fraud prevention, how to identify and communicate risk and evidence of non-compliance, and the importance of accurate record keeping.
The technical, investment, and settlement characteristics of the main investment products, instruments, currencies, and markets, for example, warrants, bonds, and contracts for difference.
The investment products and or services within their own organisation, as well as competitor products and or services available in the market, relevant to their role.

Data analysis techniques and how to interpret and communicate role-related data effectively in different formats, for example, reviewing spreadsheets, identifying trends or anomalies, and supporting wider analysis efforts through data gathering and formatting.

The different types of internal and external stakeholders, for example, human resources, finance, tax, compliance, and risk teams, regulators, and third-party service providers, their position in the market, and the importance of building positive relationships.

Communication techniques, channels, methods, and strategies, including professional, effective, and influential communication, and the importance of adapting communication style to facilitate understanding for both financial and non-financial stakeholders.
Current data legislation, cybersecurity regulations, and organisational policies for the compliant handling of data and for working securely online.

Investment operations systems, digital tools, and technologies relevant to their role, for example, wealth management platforms, settlement systems, data analytics tools, cybersecurity protocols, automated straight-through processing systems and an awareness of the potential, and safe and responsible use, of generative AI in analytics.
Current and developing sustainability principles within investment or financial services, their products, and working practices relevant to the role.
Principles and policies of equality, diversity, and inclusion (EDI) and their impact on the organisation and stakeholders relevant to their organisation.

behaviours.detail: Acts professionally, ethically, and with integrity.
Maintains confidentiality and acts with due care and diligence.
Works flexibly and adapts to changing circumstances, including overcoming challenges and responding to setbacks.
Displays a growth mindset and uses own initiative, learning from failures and spotting opportunities.
Reflects on own practice and seeks learning opportunities for Continuing Professional Development (CPD).
Takes responsibility for their actions and is accountable for their own work and managing workload.

skills.detail: Investigate, analyse, and resolve investment-related queries or operational issues, for example, completion of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) documentation, dealing with vulnerable customer cases, and in-specie transfers in accordance with regulatory and operational requirements.

Provide operational or administrative support to individuals or teams within the area of investment, for example, preparing and delivering stakeholder reports, analysing and investigating monthly attestation reports, and preparing and uploading tax year valuations.

Work autonomously and take ownership of work and potential investment-related problems within their area, for example, monitoring the progress of assigned cases to achieve optimal outcomes for stakeholders.
Identify and communicate potential risk and or incidences of non-compliance, escalating where appropriate.
Maintain accurate and up-to-date information in relevant systems, ensuring compliance with regulations, policies, and governance requirements.
Meet internal and external Service Level Agreements (SLAs) by following structured processes accurately and efficiently.

Analyse processes, appraise strengths and weaknesses, identify areas for continuous improvement through analysis of operational data, feedback, or process performance, and recommend appropriate changes to enhance efficiency or quality.
Build and maintain professional, collaborative relationships with stakeholders.
Communicate with stakeholders using methods appropriate for the intended audience, in line with organisational expectations, including verbal, written, digital, and virtual methods.
Support or mentor colleagues, act as an escalation point, and contribute to team and wider organisational aims and objectives.
Comply with current data legislation, cybersecurity regulations, and organisational policies, including when managing and storing client data safely and securely.

Use systems, digital tools, and technologies relevant to their role and workplace, ensuring this is done safely and securely, for example, Certificateless Registry for Electronic Share Transfer (CREST), customer management systems, data reconciliation and validation systems, and transfer systems.
Apply current and developing sustainability principles and working practices relevant to their business area.

Apply principles of equality, diversity, and inclusion (EDI) policy, for example, the application of Consumer Duty principles, dealing with vulnerable customers appropriately, and using inclusive language.

reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Perform complex and specialist operational support to transactions across a wide range of financial instruments and products
banners: The below revised version of the apprenticeship standard and assessment plan is approved by Skills England under the 2025/2026 apprenticeship assessment reforms and is available for information only. It will replace the current version for new starts on 11th September with a funding band of £9,000. Until then, the current standard and end-point assessment remains in place. Further details of this and other apprenticeships in revision are available in the revisions and adjustments status report.

professionalRecognition.bodyName: Chartered Institute for Securities and Investment (CISI)

duties.dutyDetail: Contribute to the delivery of investment services ensuring that all processes are carried out in accordance with internal and external service level agreements (SLAs).

Provide operational or administrative support to teams involved in the implementation of investment strategies and decisions, for example, liaising with counterparties and investment partners to ensure the smooth transfer of customer accounts.
Assist in the preparation and delivery of internal or external stakeholder reports, ensuring accuracy and timeliness.

Maintain accurate records and consistently monitor the progress of cases to achieve optimal outcomes for the organisation and stakeholders, for example by maintaining accurate and up-to-date records of transactions and holdings in relevant systems, monitoring cash flows related to investment transactions, and ensuring sufficient funds are available for settlement.

Apply in-depth knowledge of legal and regulatory requirements to resolve or escalate standard and complex stakeholder investment related queries and other operational issues, for example, investigating and resolving trade settlement failures, possible GDPR breaches.

Follow structured processes to deliver accurate outcomes that meet Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) regulatory requirements and ensure compliance with organisational policies and procedures to minimise exposure to losses, breaches, and complaints, including Consumer Duty, Client Assets Sourcebook (CASS) rules, clear desk policy, breach reporting, anti-money laundering (AML), and fraud prevention requirements.
Analyse and communicate investment-related data to support the organisation’s objectives and identify potential risks.

Investigate, analyse, and take ownership of potential problems in line with organisational procedures, and escalate investment issues that are beyond own authority, for example by reporting breaches and suspected breaches of regulation and policy to the team leader or manager in accordance with the organisation’s escalation policy.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of the organisation by identifying areas of inefficiency within the organisation and recommending appropriate changes.
Communicate effectively and build positive relationships with internal teams and external stakeholders, for example, trading teams, portfolio management teams, brokers, and custodians.

Use a range of investment operations systems, digital tools, and technology, such as generative AI analytics and data analytics, Certificateless Registry for Electronic Share Transfer (CREST), cybersecurity software, and wealth management platforms, ensuring that this is done safely and securely.

Provide support for colleagues to resolve investment issues and act as an escalation route where appropriate, providing feedback and ensuring compliance with appropriate procedures, regulations, and legislation.
Contribute to team and wider organisation aims and objectives.
Take responsibility for personal and professional development in line with organisational expectations.
